equations.name: Bohr quantization of angular momentum

equations.name: Time-dependent Schrödinger equation
equations.summary: iℏ ∂Ψ/∂t = ĤΨ — drives this atom's state; ground-state energy scale |E₁| = 13.606 eV

equations.name: Time-independent Schrödinger (eigenvalue form)
equations.summary: Ĥψ = Eψ → eigenvalues Eₙ (eV): [-13.606]

equations.name: Time-independent Schrödinger (1-D explicit)
equations.summary: −(ℏ²/2m)ψ″ + V(r)ψ = Eψ with V(r) = −kZe²/r (Z=1); curvature set by local KE

equations.name: Hamiltonian operator
equations.summary: Ĥ = −(ℏ²/2m)∂²/∂x² + V(x); for this atom ⟨Ĥ⟩ at n=1 = -13.606 eV

equations.name: Momentum operator
equations.summary: p̂ₓ = −iℏ ∂/∂x (operator — same form for every atom)

equations.name: Position–momentum commutator
equations.summary: [x̂,p̂] = iℏ = 1.055×10⁻³⁴·i (universal foundation of QM)

equations.name: de Broglie wavelength
equations.summary: λ = h/p = 332.49 pm for the valence electron (n=1)

equations.name: Born rule (probability cloud)
equations.summary: P(x) = |ψ(x)|² — the electron cloud replaces the orbit (concept, all atoms)

equations.name: Normalization of the wavefunction
equations.summary: ∫|ψ|² dx = 1 — total probability is 1, which is what quantizes the levels

equations.name: Free-particle wavefunction
equations.summary: ψ = sin(2πx/λ), E = p²/2m = 13.606 eV (valence treated as free: λ = 332.49 pm)

equations.name: Energy quantization
equations.summary: 1 occupied levels for Z=1; spectrum Eₙ (eV): [-13.606]

equations.name: Time-evolution operator
equations.summary: |ψ(t)⟩ = e^(−iĤt/ℏ)|ψ(0)⟩ — ground state phase frequency ω = |E₁|/ℏ = 2.067×10¹⁶ rad/s

equations.name: Qubit state (superposition)
equations.summary: |ψ⟩ = α|0⟩ + β|1⟩ — a valence spin/level of this atom is one such qubit

equations.name: Normalization of a qubit
equations.summary: |α|² + |β|² = 1 (Bloch vector has unit length)

equations.name: Bloch-sphere parametrization
equations.summary: |ψ⟩ = cos(θ/2)|0⟩ + e^{iφ}sin(θ/2)|1⟩ (geometry, same for all atoms)

equations.name: Bloch vector ↔ density operator
equations.summary: ρ = ½(I + s·σ); |s|=1 pure, <1 mixed

equations.name: Pauli-X gate (bit flip / NOT)
equations.summary: X = [[0,1],[1,0]] — 180° rotation about x

equations.name: Pauli-Y gate
equations.summary: Y = [[0,−i],[i,0]] — 180° rotation about y

equations.name: Pauli-Z gate (phase flip)
equations.summary: Z = [[1,0],[0,−1]] — 180° rotation about z

equations.name: Hadamard gate
equations.summary: H = (1/√2)[[1,1],[1,−1]] — pole → equator (creates superposition)

equations.name: Phase gate (S / T / general P)
equations.summary: P(φ) = diag(1, e^{iφ}); S: φ=π/2, T: φ=π/4

equations.name: Every single-qubit gate is a rotation
equations.summary: U = e^{iα n·σ} ⇒ rotate the Bloch vector by 2α about n

equations.name: Bloch-vector precession (infinitesimal)
equations.summary: s′ = s + 2α(n×s) — the qubit twin of magnetic precession

equations.name: CNOT (controlled-NOT)
equations.summary: |A,B⟩ → |A, A⊕B⟩ — the two-qubit entangler

equations.name: Spin / energy encoding of |0⟩,|1⟩
equations.summary: |0⟩=spin-down/ground, |1⟩=spin-up/excited; this atom has 1 unpaired spin(s) to encode

equations.name: Magnetic moment of a spin
equations.summary: μ = γF → spin-only moment 1.7321 µ_B (paramagnetic)

equations.name: Larmor precession of the moment
equations.summary: μ̇ = γμ×B → at B=1 T the electron spin precesses at 2.802×10¹⁰ Hz

equations.name: Energy of a moment in a field (−μ·B)
equations.summary: U = −μ·B → Zeeman level splitting at B=1 T ≈ 1.159×10⁻⁴ eV

equations.name: Effective magnetic field
equations.summary: B_eff(z) = Bₓ ê_x + B_fict(z) ê_z — model field (needs a trap geometry)

equations.name: Coupled equations of motion
equations.summary: ż = p/m, ṗ = −d/dz(U_J − μ·B) — spin–motion coupling (needs a trap)

equations.name: Qubit-in-a-field Hamiltonian (precession)
equations.summary: Ĥ = E₀I + ℏΩ(n·σ): two levels split by 2ℏΩ ≈ 1.159×10⁻⁴ eV at B=1 T

equations.name: Mass–energy equivalence
equations.summary: E = mc² → rest energy of one H atom = 938.95 MeV

equations.name: Relativistic energy
equations.summary: γ = 1/√(1−v²/c²) = 1 for the n=1 electron (v=1% c)

equations.summary: p = γmv = 1.993×10⁻²⁴ kg·m/s for the inner electron (vs 1.993×10⁻²⁴ classical)

equations.name: Lorentz transformation
equations.summary: x′,t′ mix at γ = 1 using the inner-electron speed as the frame velocity

equations.name: Time dilation & length contraction
equations.summary: at the inner-electron speed: clock ×1, length ×0.99997

equations.name: Relativistic velocity addition
equations.summary: adding the inner-electron speed to itself: W = 4.375×10⁶ m/s (< c, not 2v)

equations.name: Spacetime interval (Minkowski)
equations.summary: ds² = dx²+dy²+dz² − c²dt² (frame-invariant geometry, all atoms)

equations.summary: F = q(E + v×B): magnetic force on the n=1 electron at B=1 T ≈ 3.505×10⁻¹³ N

equations.name: Heisenberg exchange interaction
equations.summary: H = −ΣJ·Sᵢ·Sⱼ — this atom carries spin S = 0.5 into the exchange

equations.summary: H = D·(Sᵢ×Sⱼ) — twists neighbouring spins (needs a bonded partner)

equations.summary: x′,t′ mix at γ = 1.0039 using the inner-electron speed as the frame velocity

equations.summary: adding the inner-electron speed to itself: W = 5.211×10⁷ m/s (< c, not 2v)

equations.name: Heisenberg exchange interaction
equations.summary: H = −ΣJ·Sᵢ·Sⱼ — this atom carries spin S = 0.0 into the exchange

equations.name: Model spin Hamiltonian
equations.summary: contributes Ŝ with S = 0.0 (multiplicity 1) to the molecular spin ladder

equations.summary: H = D·(Sᵢ×Sⱼ) — twists neighbouring spins (needs a bonded partner)

equations.summary: H_cluster blockade entangles atoms on the array (hardware term, partner-dependent)

equations.summary: binds 24 nucleons (12 p⁺ + 12 n⁰) against proton–proton repulsion

equations.summary: λ = h/p: this atom's valence electron has λ = 83.123 pm

equations.summary: Δx·Δp ≥ ℏ/2: confining to Δx = 4.4098 pm forces Δp ≥ 1.196×10⁻²³ kg·m/s

equations.summary: F = 1.81×10⁻⁴ N inward, binding the n=1 electron to the +13e nucleus

equations.name: Newton's 2nd law / centripetal force
equations.summary: m·v²/r = 1.81×10⁻⁴ N — exactly balances the Coulomb pull (circular orbit)

equations.name: Momentum
equations.summary: p = m·v = 2.591×10⁻²³ kg·m/s (sets the de Broglie wavelength)

equations.name: Orbital angular momentum
equations.summary: |L| = |r×p| = 1.055×10⁻³⁴ J·s ≈ ℏ for the n=1 orbit

equations.name: Position vector
equations.summary: |r| = 4.0706 pm — the n=1 Bohr radius for Z=13

equations.name: Bohr quantization of angular momentum
equations.summary: L = nℏ allowed for n = 1…3 (outer shell n=3)

equations.name: Time-dependent Schrödinger equation
equations.summary: iℏ ∂Ψ/∂t = ĤΨ — drives this atom's state; ground-state energy scale |E₁| = 2299.4 eV

equations.name: Time-independent Schrödinger (1-D explicit)
equations.summary: −(ℏ²/2m)ψ″ + V(r)ψ = Eψ with V(r) = −kZe²/r (Z=13); curvature set by local KE

equations.name: Hamiltonian operator
equations.summary: Ĥ = −(ℏ²/2m)∂²/∂x² + V(x); for this atom ⟨Ĥ⟩ at n=1 = -2299.4 eV

equations.name: Momentum operator
equations.summary: p̂ₓ = −iℏ ∂/∂x (operator — same form for every atom)

equations.name: Position–momentum commutator
equations.summary: [x̂,p̂] = iℏ = 1.055×10⁻³⁴·i (universal foundation of QM)

equations.name: de Broglie wavelength
equations.summary: λ = h/p = 76.729 pm for the valence electron (n=3)

equations.name: Born rule (probability cloud)
equations.summary: P(x) = |ψ(x)|² — the electron cloud replaces the orbit (concept, all atoms)

equations.name: Normalization of the wavefunction
equations.summary: ∫|ψ|² dx = 1 — total probability is 1, which is what quantizes the levels

equations.name: Free-particle wavefunction
equations.summary: ψ = sin(2πx/λ), E = p²/2m = 2299.4 eV (valence treated as free: λ = 76.729 pm)

equations.name: Energy quantization
equations.summary: 3 occupied levels for Z=13; spectrum Eₙ (eV): [-2299.4, -574.84, -255.48]

equations.name: Time-evolution operator
equations.summary: |ψ(t)⟩ = e^(−iĤt/ℏ)|ψ(0)⟩ — ground state phase frequency ω = |E₁|/ℏ = 3.493×10¹⁸ rad/s

equations.name: Qubit state (superposition)
equations.summary: |ψ⟩ = α|0⟩ + β|1⟩ — a valence spin/level of this atom is one such qubit

equations.name: Normalization of a qubit
equations.summary: |α|² + |β|² = 1 (Bloch vector has unit length)

equations.name: Bloch-sphere parametrization
equations.summary: |ψ⟩ = cos(θ/2)|0⟩ + e^{iφ}sin(θ/2)|1⟩ (geometry, same for all atoms)

equations.name: Bloch vector ↔ density operator
equations.summary: ρ = ½(I + s·σ); |s|=1 pure, <1 mixed

equations.name: Pauli-X gate (bit flip / NOT)
equations.summary: X = [[0,1],[1,0]] — 180° rotation about x

equations.name: Pauli-Y gate
equations.summary: Y = [[0,−i],[i,0]] — 180° rotation about y

equations.name: Pauli-Z gate (phase flip)
equations.summary: Z = [[1,0],[0,−1]] — 180° rotation about z

equations.name: Hadamard gate
equations.summary: H = (1/√2)[[1,1],[1,−1]] — pole → equator (creates superposition)

equations.name: Phase gate (S / T / general P)
equations.summary: P(φ) = diag(1, e^{iφ}); S: φ=π/2, T: φ=π/4

equations.name: Every single-qubit gate is a rotation
equations.summary: U = e^{iα n·σ} ⇒ rotate the Bloch vector by 2α about n

equations.name: Bloch-vector precession (infinitesimal)
equations.summary: s′ = s + 2α(n×s) — the qubit twin of magnetic precession

equations.name: CNOT (controlled-NOT)
equations.summary: |A,B⟩ → |A, A⊕B⟩ — the two-qubit entangler

equations.name: Spin / energy encoding of |0⟩,|1⟩
equations.summary: |0⟩=spin-down/ground, |1⟩=spin-up/excited; this atom has 1 unpaired spin(s) to encode

equations.summary: μ = γF → spin-only moment 1.7321 µ_B (paramagnetic)

equations.name: Larmor precession of the moment
equations.summary: μ̇ = γμ×B → at B=1 T the electron spin precesses at 2.802×10¹⁰ Hz

equations.name: Energy of a moment in a field (−μ·B)
equations.summary: U = −μ·B → Zeeman level splitting at B=1 T ≈ 1.159×10⁻⁴ eV

equations.name: Effective magnetic field
equations.summary: B_eff(z) = Bₓ ê_x + B_fict(z) ê_z — model field (needs a trap geometry)

equations.name: Coupled equations of motion
equations.summary: ż = p/m, ṗ = −d/dz(U_J − μ·B) — spin–motion coupling (needs a trap)

equations.name: Bohr quantization of angular momentum
equations.summary: L = nℏ allowed for n = 1…7 (outer shell n=7)

equations.name: Time-dependent Schrödinger equation

equations.summary: λ = h/p = 25.576 pm for the valence electron (n=7)

equations.name: Born rule (probability cloud)
equations.summary: P(x) = |ψ(x)|² — the electron cloud replaces the orbit (concept, all atoms)

equations.summary: ∫|ψ|² dx = 1 — total probability is 1, which is what quantizes the levels

equations.name: Free-particle wavefunction
equations.summary: ψ = sin(2πx/λ), E = p²/2m = 1.127×10⁵ eV (valence treated as free: λ = 25.576 pm)

equations.summary: Z = [[1,0],[0,−1]] — 180° rotation about z

equations.name: Phase gate (S / T / general P)
equations.summary: P(φ) = diag(1, e^{iφ}); S: φ=π/2, T: φ=π/4

equations.name: Every single-qubit gate is a rotation

equations.summary: U = e^{iα n·σ} ⇒ rotate the Bloch vector by 2α about n

equations.summary: s′ = s + 2α(n×s) — the qubit twin of magnetic precession

equations.name: Spin / energy encoding of |0⟩,|1⟩

equations.summary: B_eff(z) = Bₓ ê_x + B_fict(z) ê_z — model field (needs a trap geometry)

equations.summary: x′,t′ mix at γ = 1.9208 using the inner-electron speed as the frame velocity

equations.summary: adding the inner-electron speed to itself: W = 2.961×10⁸ m/s (< c, not 2v)

equations.summary: ds² = dx²+dy²+dz² − c²dt² (frame-invariant geometry, all atoms)

equations.summary: F = q(E + v×B): magnetic force on the n=1 electron at B=1 T ≈ 4.101×10⁻¹¹ N
